LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS SQUIRE SANDERS & DEMPSEY, LLP, AND MARK DOSKER'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF'S CENTRAL CLAIMS NECESSARILY DEPEND ON RESOLUTION OF SUBSTANTIAL QUESTIONS OF FEDERAL PATENT LAW CONCERNING THE STRENGTH, OR LACK OF STRENGTH, OF PLAINTIFF'S UNDERLYING INFRINGEMENT CLAIMS. THEREFORE, THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION OVER THE COMPLAINT AS PLEADED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF PLAINTIFF CHOOSES TO LIMIT ITSELF TO CLAIMS, NOT REQUIRING ANALYSIS OF THE STRENGTH OF ITS PATENT CLAIMS. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS MOSES & SINGER, LLP AND STEPHEN WEISS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF'S CENTRAL CLAIMS NECESSARILY DEPEND ON RESOLUTION OF SUBSTANTIAL QUESTIONS OF FEDERAL PATENT LAW CONCERNING THE STRENGTH, OR LACK OF STRENGTH, OF PLAINTIFF'S UNDERLYING INFRINGEMENT CLAIMS. THEREFORE, THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION OVER THE COMPLAINT AS PLEADED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, IF PLAINTIFF CHOOSES TO LIMIT ITSELF TO CLAIMS, NOT REQUIRING ANALYSIS OF THE STRENGTH OF ITS PATENT CLAIMS. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)
